data.title: Genuine Parts Company targets $200M annualized cost savings by 2026 with restructuring efforts
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Genuine Parts Company (NYSE:GPC) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Will Stengel emphasized the company's resilience amid challenging macroeconomic conditions, highlighting strategic investments in technology, supply chain, and employee talent. He noted that the company made over 100 acquisitions in 2024, contributing to its global reach and capabilities.
  • Total sales for 2024 reached $23.5 billion, with a 1.7% increase compared to 2023. Adjusted gross margin improved by 70 basis points, attributed to acquisitions and pricing strategies.
  • The automotive segment saw $14.8 billion in total sales for 2024, up 4% year-over-year, with significant growth in nondiscretionary repair categories. However, sales in discretionary categories declined mid-single digits.
  • The industrial segment reported $8.7 billion in total sales, a decrease of 1.4%, driven by weak market conditions and reduced customer demand.
  • CFO Bert Nappier commented on the company's focus on cost control, stating, "Our global restructuring efforts announced last year have progressed ahead of plan, delivering cost savings at the high end of expectations."

Outlook

  • Management projects adjusted diluted EPS for 2025 in the range of $7.75 to $8.25. Total sales growth is expected to be between 2% and 4% in 2025, supported by recovery in industrial and European markets in the second half of the year.
  • The company anticipates restructuring efforts to yield an additional $100M to $125M in savings in 2025, contributing to a total of $200M in annualized savings by 2026.
  • Automotive segment EBITDA margin is forecasted to remain flat to slightly up, while industrial segment EBITDA margin is expected to expand by 20 to 40 basis points year-over-year.

Financial Results

  • Fourth-quarter sales increased 3.3%, driven by acquisitions and an additional selling day in the U.S. Adjusted gross margin was 36.9%, a 50-basis-point improvement.
  • Adjusted diluted EPS for Q4 was $1.61, compared to analysts' estimates of $1.55. Revenues for Q4 were $5.71 billion, aligning with analysts' estimates.
  • Operating cash flow for 2024 was $1.3 billion, with $700 million returned to shareholders through dividends and share repurchases. Capital expenditures totaled $570 million, primarily directed toward supply chain and IT enhancements.

Q&A

  • Scot Ciccarelli, Truist, questioned the timeline for operational improvements in the U.S. automotive business. CEO Stengel responded that progress is evident in customer service metrics and inventory availability but acknowledged ongoing opportunities for improvement.
  • Chris Horvers, JPMorgan, inquired about the expected cadence of sales improvement in 2025. CFO Nappier highlighted that weak market conditions are anticipated to persist in the first half, with sequential improvement expected in the second half.
  • Michael Lasser, UBS, asked about SG&A increases. CFO Nappier attributed the rise to acquisitions, inflation, and investments in IT but noted ongoing restructuring efforts to offset these costs.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were slightly negative, focusing on market share concerns, weaker-than-expected market conditions, and the impact of inflation on costs.
  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, emphasizing restructuring progress and long-term growth opportunities. CEO Stengel expressed confidence in the company's ability to navigate near-term challenges, stating, "We remain focused on creating value through a balanced capital allocation strategy."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management's tone was more pragmatic, reflecting persistent macroeconomic headwinds.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2025 includes a more cautious outlook, with weaker market conditions expected to persist into the first half, compared to the prior quarter's expectation of sequential improvement.
  • Adjusted EPS for Q4 2024 declined from Q3 2024, reflecting ongoing cost pressures and market challenges.
  • Management's tone remained consistent with the previous quarter, balancing near-term challenges with confidence in long-term initiatives.

Risks and Concerns

  • Persistent weak market conditions in global industrial and European markets remain a significant headwind.
  • Inflationary pressures on wages, rents, and freight costs could impact SG&A performance.
  • Potential delays in customer capital investments and geopolitical uncertainties, including tariffs, present risks to industrial recovery.
  • Analysts raised concerns about underperformance in U.S. automotive relative to peers and the timeline for achieving synergies from recent acquisitions.

Final Takeaway

Genuine Parts Company demonstrated resilience in 2024 despite challenging market conditions, supported by strategic acquisitions, cost-saving initiatives, and investments in technology and supply chain capabilities. Management remains cautiously optimistic for 2025, projecting gradual market recovery and targeting $200 million in annualized cost savings by 2026 through restructuring efforts. While near-term pressures persist, the company is well-positioned to capitalize on long-term growth opportunities.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Capgemini targets €1.9B free cash flow for 2025 amid cautious revenue outlook
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Capgemini SE (OTCPK:CAPMF) Q4 2024

Management View

  • CEO Aiman Ezzat noted that the company faced a "weaker-than-anticipated" market in 2024, with clients refraining from increasing discretionary spending. The company's resilience was demonstrated through investments in cloud, generative AI (gen AI), and digital core services.
  • Full-year revenue was reported at €22,096 million, down 2% year-on-year at constant currency. Bookings totaled €23,821 million, reflecting a book-to-bill ratio of 1.08.
  • Operating margin for the year stood at 13.3%, stable from the prior year, attributed to a shift towards higher-value services and improved operational efficiency.
  • CFO Nive Bhagat stated that "organic free cash flow amounted to €1,961 million," meeting the annual target despite revenue declines. Normalized EPS was reported at €12.23.
  • The company highlighted strong momentum in generative AI, with bookings accounting for 5% of Q4 bookings and nearly 4% for the year. Over 150,000 employees were trained in gen AI capabilities.
  • Strategic collaborations included a partnership with NVIDIA to develop energy-efficient GPU services and the integration of AI agents to enhance operational processes for clients across utilities, logistics, and consumer goods.

Outlook

  • Revenue guidance for 2025 was set at a range of -2% to +2% at constant currency, with scope impact expected to contribute 1 to 2 percentage points.
  • CEO Ezzat emphasized cautious optimism, citing uncertain geopolitical and economic conditions. He noted that "constant currency revenue growth for H1 2025" is expected to align with Q4 2024 levels.
  • The company aims to improve operating margin by 0 to 20 basis points in 2025 and maintain strong cash flow conversion, targeting €1.9 billion in organic free cash flow.

Financial Results

  • Revenue for Q4 2024 declined by 1.1% year-on-year at constant currency, aligning with prior revised expectations. Bookings for the quarter reached €6.8 billion, yielding a book-to-bill ratio of 1.22.
  • The operating margin remained stable at 13.3% for the year despite a decline in revenue.
  • Geographically, growth improved in North America, the U.K., Asia Pacific, and Latin America, while France experienced a notable slowdown due to challenges in the manufacturing sector.
  • Financial Services and Consumer Goods showed recovery, while manufacturing contracted further, with a 6.1% decline in Q4.

Q&A

  • Mohammed Moawalla, Goldman Sachs: Asked about discretionary spending visibility and regional growth assumptions. CEO Ezzat noted that discretionary spending remains limited outside financial services, with efficiency-focused initiatives driving demand. He cited "uncertainty on the global macroeconomic side" influencing geographic trends.
  • Balajee Tirupati, Citi: Inquired about inorganic contributions and generative AI implications. Ezzat clarified that M&A contributions are expected to range from 1 to 2 points. He highlighted gen AI's dual role in improving productivity and unlocking new business opportunities.
  • Frederic Boulan, Bank of America: Questioned free cash flow resilience amid revenue declines. CFO Bhagat attributed the performance to "financial discipline" and effective working capital management.
  • Charlie Brennan, Jefferies: Asked about pricing pressures and competition. Ezzat acknowledged some pricing pressure but emphasized operational efficiencies and a favorable portfolio mix.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious skepticism, focusing on discretionary spending, regional variability, and the impact of generative AI on client budgets. Questions reflected concerns about near-term growth visibility and competitive positioning.
  • Management maintained a cautiously optimistic tone, highlighting resilience and strategic focus on high-value services like AI and digital transformation. Ezzat's repeated emphasis on "uncertainty" and "caution" underscored a measured outlook.
  • Compared to Q3 2024, management's tone displayed increased realism about short-term headwinds, while analysts were more probing on growth sustainability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue guidance for 2025 reflected a wider range (-2% to +2%) compared to Q3’s narrowed guidance for Q4 2024 (-1.5% to -2%).
  • Manufacturing sector headwinds intensified in Q4, with a 6.1% decline compared to Q3’s 3.4% drop.
  • Management sentiment in Q4 was more cautious, highlighting continued macroeconomic uncertainties. Analysts' focus shifted from operational resilience to growth prospects and pricing pressures.

Risks and Concerns

  • Persistent challenges in the manufacturing sector and reduced discretionary spending remain key risks.
  • Geopolitical and economic uncertainties could affect client budgets, particularly in Europe and manufacturing-heavy regions like France.
  • Analysts raised concerns about margin improvement feasibility amid rising operational costs and pricing pressures.
  • Management identified opportunities to enhance sales effectiveness and leverage AI-driven efficiencies to mitigate risks.

Final Takeaway

Capgemini demonstrated resilience in a challenging market environment by maintaining stable margins and achieving strong cash flow despite revenue declines. Investments in generative AI and high-value services positioned the company for long-term growth, but near-term challenges in manufacturing and discretionary spending persist. Management’s cautious outlook for 2025 reflects ongoing geopolitical and economic uncertainties, balanced by strategic initiatives to streamline operations and enhance sales efficiency.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Tariffs might have greater impact on AI capex than DeepSeek – GS
data.description:
Changes in AI-related capital expenditures could see limited near-term implications despite DeepSeek scares, according to Jan Hatzius, chief economist and head of global investment research at Goldman Sachs.

He said that competition could “catalyze higher levels of real hardware spending if it pushes incumbents to invest more to maintain their lead.”

Goldman Sachs analysts have actually increased their estimates of 2025 AI-related investment on net over the last quarter.

Capex expectations for 2025 were revised up by 5% for S&P 1500 (NYSEARCA:SPTM) companies over the last quarter but were only revised up by 2% for companies with broad exposure to tariffs and revised down by 1% for companies with a high reported share of sales to Canada, Mexico, and China, Hatzius said.

In addition, Goldman economists said that AI-related investment have had, so far, limited impact on official GDP statistics, “thereby limiting downside to measured growth even if investment does slow,” he said.

Some companies are planning to delay AI-related capex until there is more certainty about tariffs.

During its 2024 fourth quarter earnings call, General Motors’ (GM) management said, “What we won't do is spend a large amount of capital without clarity [about potential tariffs].”

Here is what other companies said about the effect of tariffs:

  • Three Part Advisors – “Some of these projects are dependent on both the cost of the capital, but also tariffs could impact supply cost of steel. So, you now have some projects [where] the escalator clauses are being negotiated.”
  • Coty (COTY) – “In this varying macro environment, [which includes] potential tariff wars...we think that it is no longer prudent to put specific sales growth targets.”

Other companies are planning to pass along costs to consumers:

  • AMETEK (AME) – “We plan to pass on the cost impact of the tariffs, if the tariffs get enacted, to our customers, as we have done previously.”
  • Cardinal Health (CAH) – “If there are widespread tariffs, anywhere from the 10% to 25% range, I anticipate there will be corresponding price increases. We will do what we can to minimize those, but with 1% to 2% margins, we will not absorb whatever impacts are left.”
  • W.W. Grainger (GWW) – “Depending on what happens in the marketplace, the competitive environment, we would typically try to pass on and keep the same margins in what we pass on.”

Lastly, some companies have limited plans to reshore production, frontloading imports ahead of tariffs:

  • Leggett & Platt (LEG) – “Our teams continue to analyze multiple tariff scenarios, are qualifying alternative suppliers, and are evaluating potential geographic shifts in production.”
  • Taylor Morrison Home (TMHC) – “Given the steps we have taken to...reshore products and strengthen our supply chain resiliency in recent years, we believe we are prepared for any potential disruptions.”
  • MSC Industrial (MSM) – “We've taken a series of actions, including stocking inventory of our highest turned products that would be impacted by tariffs.”
  • Zebra Technologies (ZBRA) – “Our teams are doing everything they can to pull volume in from a purchasing perspective and get that inventory into the US as early as possible ahead of potential tariff impact.”

data.title: NeoGenomics plummets on Q4 2024 bottom line miss, soft EPS guidance
data.description:
• NeoGenomics (NASDAQ:NEO) is down 22% after reporting Q4 2024 financial results that missed on the bottom line. Also, the company's net loss for full-year 2024 was worse than expected.
  • The company also reiterated its 2025 guidance. It sees revenue of $735M-$745M. Consensus is $735.01M. NeoGenomics forecasts adjusted EPS of $0.15 to $0.19. Consensus is $0.20.
  • Despite a year-over-year increase in revenue in Q4, the company was hurt by an increase in operating expenses.
  • NeoGenomics ended 2024 with ~$367M in cash and cash equivalents, compared to ~$342.5M at the end of 2023.

data.title: Trump antitrust regulators leave Biden-era merger guidelines in effect
data.description:
The antitrust regulators under President Donald Trump announced they will leave merger guidelines initiated under former President Joe Biden's administration in effect.

The Federal Trade Commission and Department of Justice's joint 2023 merger guidelines are in effect and will serve as the framework for the FTC’s merger-review analysis, the FTC said in a statement on Tuesday.

“Stability is good for the enforcement agencies," FTC Chairman Andrew Ferguson said in the statement. The wholesale rescission and reworking of guidelines is time consuming and expensive. We should undertake this process sparingly. We have limited resources to patrol the beat and constant turnover undermines agency credibility.”

Late last month, DOJ filed to challenge Hewlett Packard Enterprises (HPE) planned $14 billion acquisition of Juniper Networks (JNPR). Some investors had expected the DOJ may approve the deal under the new Trump administration, which some thought would be more business friendly than Biden's.

"If merger guidelines change with every new administration, they will become largely worthless to businesses and the courts," Ferguson said in a memo to staff. "No business can plan forthe future on the basis of guidelines they know are one election away from rescission, and no court will rely on guidance that is so obviously partisan."

data.title: Wedbush announces that it is entering the ever-growing ETF industry
data.description:
Wedbush made a splash on Tuesday with the announcement that it was entering the ever-growing exchange-traded fund market.

With more than 7K ETFs listed globally, collectively managing over $10T in assets, the ETF market continues to be one of the fastest-growing sectors in investment management. Both actively managed and rules-based index ETFs are seeing increased adoption among institutional and retail investors alike.

As a result, Wedbush said it will sponsor its own ETFs as it aims to provide market participants with investment options designed to meet modern portfolio construction needs.

“ETF creation is a logical progression for Wedbush as we continue to provide efficient solutions to our investor clients,” said Gary Wedbush, CEO of the Los Angeles-based securities firm.

“For nearly 70 years, we’ve steadily grown Wedbush by leveraging the expertise of our colleagues and our cutting-edge technology and operational infrastructure. It’s our vision that Wedbush will become the platform of choice for entrepreneurial ETF managers,” Wedbush added.

As Wedbush looks to enter the competitive ETF landscape, see below how the market’s top 20 funds rank among each other from an assets under management perspective:

No. 20: Vanguard Total International Stock ETF (VXUS)

No. 19: SPDR Gold Shares (GLD)

No. 18: iShares Core MSCI Emerging Markets ETF (IEMG)

No. 17: Vanguard FTSE Emerging Markets ETF (VWO)

No. 16: Vanguard Information Technology ETF (VGT)

No. 15: iShares Core S&P Small-Cap ETF (IJR)

No. 14: Vanguard Dividend Appreciation ETF (VIG)

No. 13: iShares Core S&P Mid-Cap ETF (IJH)

No. 12: iShares Russell 1000 Growth ETF (IWF)

No. 11: iShares Core U.S. Aggregate Bond ETF (AGG)

No. 10: Vanguard Total Bond Market ETF (BND)

No. 9: iShares Core MSCI EAFE ETF (IEFA)

No. 8: Vanguard Value ETF (VTV)

No. 7: Vanguard FTSE Developed Markets ETF (VEA)

No. 6: Vanguard Growth ETF (VUG)

No. 5: Invesco QQQ Trust Series I (QQQ)

No. 4: Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF (VTI)

No. 3: iShares Core S&P 500 ETF (NYSEARCA:IVV)

No. 2: Vanguard S&P 500 ETF (NYSEARCA:VOO)

No. 1: SPDR S&P 500 ETF Trust (NYSEARCA:SPY)

data.title: TotalEnergies, Eni ink deal to bring Cyprus offshore gas to Europe via Egypt
data.description:
TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) and Eni (NYSE:E) said Monday they signed an agreement to export gas offshore Cyprus via Egypt, hailing the deal as a "milestone" towards the creation of a gas hub in the eastern Mediterranean.

TotalEnergies (NYSE:TTE) and Eni (NYSE:E), which each own a 50% interst in the Cronos Block 6 site offshore Cyprus, said gas from the site will be processed at Egypt's Zohr facilities before being liquefied at the Damietta liquefied natural gas plant and exported to Europe.

The companies estimate gas in place at Cronos at more than 3T cf, with Block 6 encompassing further potential resources under exploration and appraisal.

"This project leverages Egypt's existing infrastructure, including export facilities, which are a key enabler for developments in the region," Eni (E) CEO Claudio Descalzi said.

Egypt had sought to become a major gas exporter after Eni (E) discovered the Zohr offshore field in 2015, but Egypt's gas production has been falling since 2021.

data.title: S&P wobbles near record high as investors look for firm direction in 'overvalued' stock market
data.description:
Major U.S. equity market averages wavered Tuesday as investors kicked off the holiday-shortened trading week with attention on geopolitics, and sought firm direction with stocks near or at record highs.

The S&P 500 (SP500) was near even, circling near its record high of 6,128.18 notched in late January. The Dow (DJI) was -0.2%. The Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) was also near even, while the Nasdaq-100 (NDX) reached a record high of 22,191.37 before slipping.

The tech-concentrated Nasdaq-100 (NDX) has gained more than 5% this year. “[An] impressive feat considering the volatility from DeepSeek, tariffs, and interest rates,” Adam Turnquist, chief technical strategist at LPL Financial, said in a Tuesday note. The S&P 500 (SP500) has advanced 4% so far this year.

Tuesday's gainers included Intel (INTC), up +9 after a Wall Street Journal report that Taiwan Semiconductor (TSM) and Broadcom (AVGO) are separately looking at acquiring parts of the chipmaker.

As equities march higher, nine in 10 global money managers consider U.S. stocks as overvalued, according to Bank of America's latest survey. The survey of 205 panelists with $482B in assets under management, showed that 89% of respondents see U.S. stocks (SPY)(QQQ)(DIA) as overpriced, the most since at least April 2001.

In the bond market, global rates rose. European bonds were the driver, with prices falling on speculation that the market would see new issuance as countries spent more on defense.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 4 basis points to 4.53% and the 2-year yield (US2Y) was up 3 basis points to 4.29%.

U.S. and Russia talks over ending Russia's war against Ukraine began Tuesday in Saudi Arabia. Ukraine and European Union nations were not present.

Deutsche Bank strategist Jim Reid said "potential implications are huge after the events of the last five days where first the US froze out Europe in announcing the start of talks with Russia, and second with the broadside against Europe from US Vice President JD Vance."

"That all this is occurring within a week of the German general election (this Sunday) means that Europe might start to move down a very different path within weeks to the pre-existing one," he said.

On the macro front, the February Empire State Manufacturing Index surprised to the upside.

"The initial reaction (Monday) was to price in more European defense spending with bonds selling off and equities rising, especially in the defense sector," Reid said.

data.title: Medtronic targets $1B cardiovascular growth in FY25 with PFA expansion
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Medtronic (NYSE:MDT) Q3 2025

Management View

  • CEO Geoff Martha highlighted mid-single-digit revenue growth for the ninth consecutive quarter, driven by strong performances in cardiac ablation solutions, leadless pacing, neuromodulation, and diabetes. He emphasized the significant potential of renal denervation and stated, "We are stacking growth drivers on top of growth drivers with groundbreaking innovation in some of the most attractive markets in MedTech."

  • The cardiovascular portfolio grew mid-single-digits, with cardiac ablation solutions reporting 22% growth, driven by the Pulse Field Ablation (PFA) products, Affera and PulseSelect. Management anticipates this portfolio to reach $1 billion in sales for FY25 and sees a path to $2 billion as PFA adoption expands globally.

  • Structural heart products, including the Evolut FX+ TAVR system, showed high-single-digit growth excluding congenital issues. Upcoming data presentations, such as the five-year low-risk data and two-year SMART trial results, are expected to further strengthen this segment.

  • The neuroscience portfolio grew mid-single-digits, with cranial and spinal technologies achieving 5% growth globally and 8% in the U.S. Neuromodulation grew 13%, supported by closed-loop sensing technology in pain stim and brain modulation devices.

  • CFO Gary Corona reported Q3 revenue of $8.3 billion with adjusted EPS of $1.39. He noted, "The EPS beat was driven by better-than-expected operating profit on stronger gross margins and a better-than-expected tax rate."

Outlook

  • Management reiterated FY25 organic revenue growth guidance of 4.75% to 5% and non-GAAP diluted EPS guidance of $5.44 to $5.50. Q4 revenue and EPS growth are expected to accelerate, with cardiovascular portfolio growth driving the momentum.

  • The company anticipates high-single-digit EPS growth in the back half of FY25, with strong operating margin expansion.

  • Geoff Martha expressed confidence in the renal denervation opportunity, citing Medicare coverage expected within eight months and a large addressable U.S. patient population.

Financial Results

  • Q3 revenue reached $8.3 billion, representing 4.1% organic growth. Adjusted EPS was $1.39 compared to analysts' estimate of $1.36.

  • Gross margin improved to 66.6%, aided by cost efficiency programs, disciplined pricing, and favorable business mix.

  • Neuroscience and cardiovascular portfolios contributed mid-single-digit growth, offsetting slower performance in medical surgical due to U.S. distributor dynamics.

  • Operating margin rose to 26.2%, up 100 basis points year-over-year, reflecting operational improvements and cost controls.

Q&A

  • Patrick Wood, Morgan Stanley: Questioned FY26 growth trajectory. Geoff Martha affirmed commitment to profitable growth, reiterating FY25 guidance of mid-single-digit revenue and high-single-digit EPS growth.

  • Larry Biegelsen, Wells Fargo: Asked about cardiac ablation solutions growth drivers and Affera ramp-up. Sean Salmon stated, "Affera’s integrated mapping and ablation capabilities are driving high demand... The new factory in Galway has significantly improved supply."

  • Travis Steed, Bank of America: Inquired about balancing growth investments and margin expansion. Geoff Martha emphasized gross margin improvements and cost controls as key enablers for sustained investment in growth drivers.

  • Vijay Kumar, Evercore ISI: Asked about Affera revenue potential. Sean Salmon noted, "Revenue per case is higher due to additional components like mapping catheters, offering hospitals cost savings with integrated solutions."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed skepticism regarding U.S. surgical performance and distributor inventory dynamics but were optimistic about cardiovascular and neuromodulation growth drivers.

  • Management maintained a confident tone, particularly in prepared remarks, emphasizing operational improvements, supply chain stability, and product innovation. Geoff Martha frequently highlighted "stacking growth drivers" and "disciplined pricing." However, some defensive language appeared in responses to surgical business inquiries.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management showed increased confidence in cardiovascular growth and PFA adoption.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q3 saw continued mid-single-digit revenue growth, consistent with Q2. However, Q3 highlighted stronger cardiovascular performance, with PFA sales accelerating.

  • Q3 adjusted EPS growth of 6.9% marked an improvement over Q2’s 8% constant currency EPS growth, reflecting operational leverage and margin expansion.

  • Management’s tone was more optimistic regarding cardiovascular and diabetes segments, while surgical performance challenges persisted.

Risks and Concerns

  • U.S. surgical portfolio faced headwinds from distributor inventory adjustments, impacting performance. Management expects this to normalize in Q1 FY26.

  • Peripheral vascular business declined due to China’s volume-based procurement reforms, introducing volatility.

  • Analysts raised concerns about new growth drivers' potential impact on margins, given early-stage investments in Hugo robotics and Affera ablation systems.

Final Takeaway

Medtronic reported strong Q3 results, driven by cardiovascular and neuromodulation growth. Management emphasized the significant potential of PFA and renal denervation, targeting $1 billion in FY25 cardiovascular sales. With high-single-digit EPS growth expected in the second half, the company remains focused on operational leverage, disciplined pricing, and innovation to sustain long-term growth.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Meta announces new developer conference LlamaCon due to 'unprecedented growth'
data.description:
Meta Platforms (NASDAQ:META) announced a new developer conference dubbed LlamaCon, which is scheduled for April 29.

Meta's Llama model was downloaded more than 650M times in 2024, and Meta AI reached about 600M average daily users by the end of the year, the social media company reported. The surge in popularity prompted the Mark Zuckerberg-led company to create LlamaCon.

"Following the unprecedented growth and momentum of our open-source Llama collection of models and tools, we're excited to introduce LlamaCon—a developer conference for 2025 that will take place April 29," Meta said.

Meta plans to reveal its latest open source, artificial intelligence-powered tools to help developers build apps and other products at LlamaCon.

The company also announced the date of this year's Meta Connect, which is slated for September 17-18.

"As in years past, we'll share the latest and greatest in Meta Horizon updates, peel back the curtain on tomorrow's tech, and give XR devs the tools they need to help build for the next computing platform today," the company said.

Last year, Meta revealed its Meta Quest 3S virtual reality headset.
